title: मधुराष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मधुराष्टकम् ॥

॥ श्री वल्लभाचार्य विरचितम् ॥

अधरं मधुरं वदनं मधुरं
नयनं मधुरं हसितं मधुरम्।
हृदयं मधुरं गमनं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ १॥

वचनं मधुरं चरितं मधुरं
वसनं मधुरं वलितं मधुरम्।
चलितं मधुरं भ्रमितं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ २॥

वेणुर्मधुरो रेणुर्मधुरः
पाणिर्मधुरः पादौ मधुरौ।
नृत्यं मधुरं सख्यं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ ३॥

गीतं मधुरं पीतं मधुरं
भुक्तं मधुरं सुप्तं मधुरम्।
रूपं मधुरं तिलकं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ ४॥

करणं मधुरं तरणं मधुरं
हरणं मधुरं स्मरणं मधुरम्।
वमितं मधुरं शमितं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ ५॥

गुञ्जा मधुरा माला मधुरा
यमुना मधुरा वीची मधुरा।
सलिलं मधुरं कमलं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ ६॥

गोपी मधुरा लीला मधुरा
युक्तं मधुरं मुक्तं मधुरम्।
दृष्टं मधुरं शिष्टं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ ७॥

गोपा मधुरा गावो मधुरा
यष्टिर्मधुरा सृष्टिर्मधुरा।
दलितं मधुरं फलितं मधुरं
मधुराधिपतेरखिलं मधुरम्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्वल्लभाचार्यविरचितं मधुराष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Madhurashtakam ||

|| Shri Vallabhacharya Virachitam ||

Adharam madhuram vadanam madhuram
Nayanam madhuram hasitam madhuram |
Hridayam madhuram gamanam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 1 ||

Vachanam madhuram charitam madhuram
Vasanam madhuram valitam madhuram |
Chalitam madhuram bhramitam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 2 ||

Venur-madhuro renur-madhurah
Panir-madhurah padau madhurau |
Nrityam madhuram sakhyam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 3 ||

Gitam madhuram pitam madhuram
Bhuktam madhuram suptam madhuram |
Rupam madhuram tilakam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 4 ||

Karanam madhuram taranam madhuram
Haranam madhuram smaranam madhuram |
Vamitam madhuram shamitam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 5 ||

Gunja madhura mala madhura
Yamuna madhura vichi madhura |
Salilam madhuram kamalam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 6 ||

Gopi madhura lila madhura
Yuktam madhuram muktam madhuram |
Drishtam madhuram shishtam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 7 ||

Gopa madhura gavo madhura
Yashtir-madhura srishtir-madhura |
Dalitam madhuram phalitam madhuram
Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram || 8 ||

|| Iti Shrimad-Vallabhacharya-virachitam Madhurashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
उनके अधर (होंठ) मधुर हैं, उनका वदन (मुख) मधुर है, उनके नयन मधुर हैं, उनकी हँसी मधुर है। उनका हृदय मधुर है, उनकी चाल मधुर है — मधुराधिपति (मथुरा के स्वामी श्रीकृष्ण) का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ १॥

उनका वचन मधुर है, उनका चरित्र मधुर है, उनका वस्त्र मधुर है, उनका करवट लेना मधुर है। उनका चलना मधुर है, उनका भ्रमण मधुर है — मधुराधिपति का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ २॥

उनकी वेणु (बाँसुरी) मधुर है, उनकी रेणु (धूल) मधुर है, उनके हाथ मधुर हैं, उनके पैर मधुर हैं। उनका नृत्य मधुर है, उनकी मित्रता मधुर है — मधुराधिपति का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ ३॥

उनका गीत मधुर है, उनका पीना मधुर है, उनका खाना मधुर है, उनका सोना मधुर है। उनका रूप मधुर है, उनका तिलक मधुर है — मधुराधिपति का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ ४॥

उनका करना मधुर है, उनका तरना (उद्धार) मधुर है, उनका हरना मधुर है, उनका स्मरण मधुर है। उनका कहना मधुर है, उनका शमन मधुर है — मधुराधिपति का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ ५॥

गुञ्जा (घुँघची) मधुर है, माला मधुर है, यमुना मधुर है, लहर मधुर है। जल मधुर है, कमल मधुर है — मधुराधिपति का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ ६॥

गोपी मधुर है, लीला मधुर है, योग मधुर है, मोक्ष मधुर है। दृष्टि मधुर है, शिष्टता मधुर है — मधुराधिपति का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ ७॥

ग्वाले मधुर हैं, गायें मधुर हैं, लाठी मधुर है, सृष्टि मधुर है। दलित (दमित) मधुर है, फलित मधुर है — मधुराधिपति का सब कुछ मधुर है॥ ८॥
benefits: Deepens love and devotion for Lord Krishna
Fills the heart with divine sweetness and joy
Traditionally recited for removal of mental stress and brings inner peace
Purifies the mind and elevates consciousness
Attracts divine grace and blessings of Sri Krishna
Helps in developing Bhakti Rasa (devotional sentiment)
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Krishna, Madhurashtakam (मधुराष्टकम्) stands as an eight-verse devotional hymn. Drawn from Shri Vallabhacharya, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to purify the mind and elevate consciousness, fill the heart with divine sweetness and joy, and deepen love and devotion for Lord Krishna. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar), Sunday (Ravivar), and Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami and Ekadashi for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Madhurashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Madhurashtakam (Adharam Madhuram) by Vallabhacharya in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Devotional hymn to Lord Krishna.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Madhurashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Madhurashtakam (मधुराष्टकम्, literally Eight Verses on Sweetness) is one of the most beloved short Sanskrit hymns to Lord Krishna, composed by Sri Vallabhacharya Mahaprabhu (1479-1531 CE) — the founder of the Pushtimarga (Path of Divine Grace) sampradaya and one of the four principal Acharyas of medieval Vaishnavism alongside Ramanuja, Madhva and Nimbarka. The hymn consists of eight verses (ashtakam) in which every line declares that some aspect of Madhuradhipati (the Lord of Madhura / Mathura — that is, Krishna) is madhuram (sweet). The signature refrain — Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram (everything about the Lord of Madhura is sweet) — closes each verse. The Madhurashtakam systematically catalogues the all-encompassing madhurya (sweetness) of Krishna: his lips, face, eyes, smile, words, conduct, walk, flute, dust, hands, feet, dance, friendship, ornaments, even the gopis around him, the Yamuna, the lotuses, his very destruction and creation — everything is sweet. (Source: Vallabhacharya Madhurashtakam; Pushtimarga corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Vallabhacharya and what is the Pushtimarga sampradaya?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Vallabhacharya Mahaprabhu (1479-1531 CE) was a Telugu Vaishnava saint-philosopher born at Champaranya in present-day Chhattisgarh during his Tailanga Brahmin family's pilgrimage. By age 11 he had defeated all opponents in a great philosophical debate at Vijayanagara under Krishnadevaraya. He travelled across India three times establishing eighty-four sitting-places (baithakas) where he expounded the Bhagavata Purana. His philosophy — Shuddhadvaita (Pure Non-Dualism) — distinguishes itself from Shankara's Advaita by holding the world to be a real manifestation of Brahman rather than maya. His sampradaya, known as Pushtimarga (the Path of Pushti / Divine Grace), centres on the worship of Sri Krishna as Shrinathji (Govardhan-dhari) at Nathdwara in Rajasthan, and emphasises seva (loving service) of Krishna in the modes of Bal-Krishna and Kishora-Krishna. The Madhurashtakam is the supreme dhyana-stotra of the Pushtimarga, recited daily before every seva. (Sources: Vallabhacharya biographical tradition; Pushtimarga Sampradaya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Madhuradhipati and the refrain Akhilam Madhuram?
seoFaqs.answer: Madhuradhipati = Madhura + adhipati = the Lord (adhipati) of Madhura. Madhura has a beautiful double meaning: (1) the city of Mathura — Krishna's birthplace and the kingdom he ruled after slaying Kamsa, hence Madhuradhipati = the Lord of Mathura, that is, Krishna; (2) madhura = sweet, honeyed — hence Madhuradhipati = the Lord of all that is sweet. Vallabhacharya's poetic genius is to use this double-meaning to fuse the historical Krishna of Mathura with the supreme rasa of madhurya (divine sweetness). The refrain — Madhuradhipater-akhilam madhuram — means: everything (akhilam) about the Lord of Madhura / the Lord of Sweetness is itself sweet (madhuram). Each verse first names six or seven specific attributes (lips, face, eyes...) declaring each as madhuram, and then the refrain generalises: since the source is sweetness itself, everything emerging from him — every limb, every action, every association — is also sweetness. (Source: Vallabhacharya Madhurashtakam.)

seoFaqs.question: What aspects of Krishna does the Madhurashtakam declare to be sweet?
seoFaqs.answer: The eight verses systematically catalogue Krishna's all-pervading madhurya. Verse 1 — lips, face, eyes, smile, heart, gait. Verse 2 — speech, conduct, garments, the way of turning, walking, wandering. Verse 3 — flute (venu), dust of his feet (renu), hands, feet, dance, friendship. Verse 4 — songs, drink, food, sleep, form, tilak. Verse 5 — actions, deliverance (taranam), stealing of hearts (haranam), remembrance, pacifying. Verse 6 — gunja-berry necklace, garlands, the Yamuna river, its waves, its waters, the lotuses. Verse 7 — the gopis, the leelas, what is appropriate, what is liberated, what is seen, what is fitting. Verse 8 — the gopas (cowherds), the cows (gavah), the herder's stick (yashti), the very creation (srishti), what is destroyed, what is fruited. The progression moves from Krishna's body, to his actions, to his ornaments and surroundings, to his entire universe — culminating in the realisation that for the loving devotee even the destruction (dalitam) and the manifestation (phalitam) of the cosmos are sweet because they all emerge from the Sweet One. (Source: Vallabhacharya Madhurashtakam verses 1-8.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Madhurashtakam be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Madhurashtakam is most powerfully recited on Thursday (Guruvar — sacred to Krishna and to the Pushtimarga line of acharyas), Sunday (Ravivar) and Wednesday (Budhvar — sacred to Krishna), and especially on Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — the supreme festival of Krishna's birth, when the Madhurashtakam is sung throughout the night-vigil), Ekadashi of every fortnight (especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi, Mokshada Ekadashi, Devshayani and Devuthani Ekadashi), Radha Ashtami, Govardhan Puja / Annakut, Sharad Purnima (the night of the maha-rasa), Holi, Vasant Panchami, and on Vallabha Jayanti (Vaishakha Krishna Ekadashi — the birth-anniversary of Vallabhacharya). In Pushtimarga households and havelis (especially Shrinathji Nathdwara, Dwarkadheesh Kankroli, Vitthalnathji Nathdwara) the Madhurashtakam is recited at every shringar darshan and at the conclusion of every seva. Daily morning recitation before the Bal Krishna or Shrinathji murti is the classical practice. (Sources: Pushtimarga seva paddhati; Vaishnava festival calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Madhurashtakam grant to devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Madhurashtakam: (1) awakening of madhurya-bhakti — the supreme rasa of devotional love that experiences Krishna as the all-pervading source of sweetness; (2) purification of the heart through devotional poetry — the very act of repeating madhuram, madhuram saturates the mind with the rasa of sweetness; (3) absorption in Krishna-smarana (continuous remembrance of Krishna) — the mind that recites this hymn becomes incapable of dwelling on anything but Krishna; (4) entry into the Pushti-bhakti-marga — the path of divine grace where Krishna himself draws the devotee through pushti (nourishment); (5) experience of bhakti-rasa-sundara — the beautiful relish of devotional aesthetic emotion, especially the bhavas of vatsalya (parental love towards Bal-Krishna) and madhurya (the beloved-rasa); (6) protection through Krishna's grace; (7) ultimate residence in Krishna's eternal Vaikuntha-vrindavana. (Source: Pushtimarga bhakti tradition; Vallabhacharya commentaries on Bhagavata.)
